howItWorks.intro: Turkey hunting seasons are set annually by each state's wildlife agency to manage wild turkey populations sustainably.
rules: Spring season: generally March–May, targeting gobblers (male turkeys) with beards
Fall season: typically October–January, and some states allow either-sex harvest
Bag limits: vary from 1 to 4+ turkeys per season depending on the state and population
Legal birds (spring): most states restrict spring harvest to bearded turkeys (gobblers)
Hunting hours: typically sunrise to sunset or 30 minutes before sunrise to sunset
Many states split their season into periods (youth, archery-only, general) with different dates
howItWorks.outro: Dates shown are approximate general season dates. Always verify exact dates with your state wildlife agency before hunting. Youth seasons, special hunts, and zone-specific dates may differ.

faq.question: When is spring turkey season?
faq.answer: Spring turkey season generally runs from mid-March through late May, depending on the state. Southern states tend to open earlier (March), while northern states open later (April-May). Most spring seasons last 4-6 weeks.

faq.question: What is the bag limit for turkey?
faq.answer: Bag limits vary widely by state, typically 1-4 birds per season. Spring seasons usually allow 1-2 gobblers. Fall seasons may allow additional birds, and some states have generous either-sex limits. Always check your state's current regulations.

faq.question: Can you hunt turkey hens in the spring?
faq.answer: In most states, spring turkey hunting is restricted to bearded turkeys only (primarily gobblers). A small percentage of hens have beards and are legal. Some states have limited spring either-sex hunts, but this is uncommon.

faq.question: Do all states have a fall turkey season?
faq.answer: No. Some states like Texas, California, and several others do not offer a fall turkey season or only offer it in limited areas. Fall seasons are more common in eastern states with strong turkey populations.

faq.question: What permits do I need to hunt turkey?
faq.answer: At minimum, you need a valid hunting license and a turkey permit or tag for the state. Some states require completion of a turkey hunting education course. Public land may require additional access permits. Federal land may have separate regulations.

faq.question: Can I use a rifle to hunt turkey?
faq.answer: Regulations vary by state and season. Spring seasons typically allow shotguns and archery only. Some states allow rifles or crossbows in fall. Shotgun gauge and shot size restrictions are common. Always check your state's legal methods.
